name: Tailscale
what_it_does: Zero-config mesh VPN built on WireGuard that lets devices, servers, and services connect securely without port forwarding or firewall rules, with a REST API and official MCP server for network management automation.
use_cases: Giving AI agents secure access to internal services without exposing them to the internet
Automating device enrollment and ACL policy management via API
Building network topology awareness into agents using the Tailscale API
Rotating auth keys and managing device expiry programmatically
Querying which devices are online and their IP addresses for orchestration workflows
not_for: Traditional site-to-site VPN replacing hardware appliances at scale
High-throughput data transfer where WireGuard overhead matters
Organizations requiring self-hosted control plane with no SaaS dependency (use Headscale)
Anonymous or privacy-first VPN use (Tailscale sees your device graph)
best_when: You need secure, zero-config connectivity between services across cloud providers, on-prem, and developer machines, and want to automate network policy via API or MCP.
avoid_when: You need a traditional client VPN for end-user internet privacy, or your organization mandates fully self-hosted network control planes.

auth.notes: OAuth clients support fine-grained scopes (devices:read, network:read, acls:write, etc.). API keys are simpler but less scoped. OAuth is recommended for agent workflows requiring minimal privilege.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for personal use: up to 3 users, 100 devices
pricing.notes: Free personal tier is genuinely capable for individual developers and small experiments. Teams need paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits apply per tailnet; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error details. Auth scope errors are descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device registration is idempotent via auth keys. ACL updates replace the full policy document — partial updates require read-modify-write.
known_agent_gotchas: ACL policy updates replace the entire policy — agents must read existing policy before modifying to avoid accidental lockouts
Auth keys have expiry and device count limits — track key usage when automating device enrollment
MCP server is official but newer — verify capabilities before production use
Device approval may be required in your tailnet before an enrolled device can communicate
API operates on tailnet slug, not numeric ID — ensure you're using the correct identifier format

security_score_components.security_notes: WireGuard-based VPN — zero-trust networking. API keys with read/write scopes. OAuth2 for automation. SOC2 Type II. Tailscale's ACLs define which nodes can communicate. Auth keys for device provisioning in agent automation. MagicDNS for service discovery.
